text:
Once in a sleepy town named Whimsy lived a fox named Flick. Flick loved to explore. One autumn day, Flick went out late into the woods to find some wild apples. As he trekked through the damp foliage, he heard a loud crunching noise.

Looking down, Flick saw his foot had accidentally stepped on a branch. He leaped! Unfortunately, his sharp fangs got hooked into something prickly. A branch tangled his ankles.

Pain shot through his paw. Flick tried to extricate himself. But something strange happened—his fur began to glow dimly.

He could hear the forest creatures murmur softly. "Do not harm the flicker."

After what felt like ages, Flick wriggled free. The moonlight danced across his glowing spots, turning him into something magical—perhaps a guardian.

From that night on, Flick stayed close to the woods. He watched over the creatures with that magical glow. From hummingbirds
